2604.09162.title: Persona-E$^2$: A Human-Grounded Dataset for Personality-Shaped Emotional Responses to Textual Events
2604.09162.paper_summary: Most affective computing research treats emotion as a static property of text, focusing on the writer's sentiment while overlooking the reader's perspective. This approach ignores how individual personalities lead to diverse emotional appraisals of the same event. Although role-playing Large Language Models (LLMs) attempt to simulate such nuanced reactions, they often suffer from "personality illusion'' -- relying on surface-level stereotypes rather than authentic cognitive logic. A critical bottleneck is the absence of ground-truth human data to link personality traits to emotional shifts. To bridge the gap, we introduce Persona-E$^2$ (Persona-Event2Emotion), a large-scale dataset grounded in annotated MBTI and Big Five traits to capture reader-based emotional variations across news, social media, and life narratives. Extensive experiments reveal that state-of-the-art LLMs struggle to capture precise appraisal shifts, particularly in social media domains. Crucially, we find that personality information significantly improves comprehension, with the Big Five traits alleviating "personality illusion.'
2604.09162.paper_summary_zh: 大多數情感計算研究將情感視為文本的靜態屬性，專注於作者的情感而忽視了讀者的視角。這種方法忽略了個人性格如何導致對同一事件的多樣情感評價。儘管角色扮演的大型語言模型（LLMs）試圖模擬這種細緻的反應，但它們常常受到「人格幻覺」的困擾——依賴於表面刻板印象而非真實的認知邏輯。一個關鍵瓶頸是缺乏真實的人類數據來將人格特徵與情感變化聯繫起來。為了填補這一空白，我們介紹了Persona-E$^2$（Persona-Event2Emotion），這是一個基於標註的MBTI和五大人格特徵的大規模數據集，旨在捕捉基於讀者的情感變化，涵蓋新聞、社交媒體和生活敘事。廣泛的實驗顯示，最先進的LLMs在捕捉精確的評價變化方面面臨挑戰，特別是在社交媒體領域。關鍵的是，我們發現人格信息顯著改善了理解，五大人格特徵減輕了「人格幻覺」。
2604.09162.author: Yuqin Yang et.al.
2604.09162.authors: Yuqin Yang, Haowu Zhou, Haoran Tu, Zhiwen Hui, Shiqi Yan, HaoYang Li, Dong She, Xianrong Yao, Yang Gao, Zhanpeng Jin
